scope.trajectory: frozen Phase 5-A Oracle NMPC closed-loop trajectory
scope.global_reference: identical Phase 5-A A* polyline for both NMPC solvers
scope.only_independent_variable: local obstacle parameters extracted from Oracle BEV versus warehouse candidate BEV

obstacle_adapter.selection: nearest forward occupied and valid BEV cell is the obstacle point; the ellipse axes are the point-robot Minkowski safety inflation

metric_definitions.all_frame_metrics: all 1000 frames remain mandatory for pairing, validity, solver success and latency
metric_definitions.excluded_from_action_equivalence: center_stop teleports into footprint contact; side_go_left/right permit multiple safe bypass directions; their perception decisions remain gated by Phase 5-C3/D/E and their continuous control behavior by Phase 5-G
metric_definitions.oracle_brake_recall: Oracle hard-brake frames have acceleration <= -0.5 m/s^2; candidate must also hard brake
metric_definitions.oracle_release_specificity: Oracle release frames have acceleration >= 0.0 m/s^2; candidate must not hard brake; moderate Oracle deceleration is excluded
metric_definitions.steering_direction_agreement: compare sign only outside center_stop and when abs(Oracle omega) >= 0.1 rad/s; center obstacle bypass side and near-zero sign are non-unique

interpretation_note: Phase 5-F is a counterfactual shadow gate, not a continuous dynamic-control gate. Phase 5-G owns physically continuous takeover acceptance.
